pattern_recognition: Sliding Window Technique, used for problems involving subarrays or subsequences where a fixed-size window slides over the input. Signals include the need to maintain a dynamic set of elements as the window changes.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check each window by iterating through it to find the maximum element.
approaches.algorithm: For each starting index of the window, iterate through the next k elements to find the maximum.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a deque to maintain the indices of the maximum elements in the current window, allowing for efficient updates as the window slides.
approaches.algorithm: Maintain a deque that stores indices of elements in a way that the front of the deque always has the index of the maximum element for the current window.

dry_run: Given nums = [1, 3, -1, -3, 5, 3, 6, 7] and k = 3: Start with the first window [1, 3, -1], max is 3. Slide to [3, -1, -3], max is 3. Slide to [-1, -3, 5], max is 5. Continue until the last window [3, 6, 7], max is 7. Final output: 3 3 5 5 6 7.
edge_cases: Empty input should return an empty output. A single element array should return that element. Duplicates should be handled correctly, ensuring the max is returned. Large inputs should be processed efficiently without timeouts. Overflow is not a concern with typical integer ranges.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not handling the case where k is larger than the array length, which should return an error or empty. 2) Failing to update the deque correctly when elements exit the window. 3) Not considering negative numbers which might affect max calculations.
